summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held its regular meeting and addressed the continued Zoning Case 2025-010 (Westbrook's Preliminary Development Plan). The commission voted to dismiss the case on the basis that the underlying 2005 zoning map amendment failed to receive the required statutory majority vote of the Board of Township Trustees, meaning no lawful rezoning ever occurred. The property reverts to R1 zoning. Property owner Marc Orlando spoke in opposition to the dismissal, expressing concerns about having purchased the property under the assumption the PUD zoning was valid.
decisions: Approved video meeting minutes from February 3, 2026 (motion by Lewis, second by Curie; passed with one abstention)

Dismissed Zoning Case ZC 2025-010 on the basis that the 2005 zoning map amendment failed to receive the statutory required majority vote of the Board of Township Trustees (motion by Pegram, second by Dinwiddie; passed 4-0 with 2 abstentions)
Meeting adjourned (motion by Curie, second by Dinwiddie; passed unanimously)
action_items: Staff noted the land use plan community meeting with Drew DeMarsh from Clermont County Planning Commission is scheduled for the next day (March 3, 2026) in the training/community room
Marc Orlando was advised to file a public records request to obtain the opinion letter and memo regarding the legality of the 2005 zoning actions
key_amounts: $2,000 - cost of independent property review referenced by Marc Orlando
notable_topics: Legality of the 2005 zoning map amendment and Board of Trustees vote (one affirmative, one abstention, one absent - failed to meet statutory majority)
Dismissal of Zoning Case ZC 2025-010 (Westbrook's Preliminary Development Plan)
Property reverts to R1 zoning classification due to improper 2005 rezoning
Property owner Marc Orlando's concerns about purchasing property based on PUD zoning that was shown on maps for 20+ years
Upcoming land use plan community meeting with Clermont County Planning Commission
attendees: Drew DeMarsh (mentioned, Clermont County Planning Commission)
notes: Marc Orlando, the property owner, expressed strong concerns that he purchased the property relying on PUD zoning that had been shown on official maps for over 20 years. He referenced meeting minutes from 2005 showing discussions at irregular hours and potential text/electronic votes, and suggested further investigation was warranted. The administrator noted that the PUD designation on the county map was controlled by Clermont County and reflected what was reported to them at the time. The Clermont County Prosecutor's Office provided a legal opinion supporting the position that the 2005 rezoning was not legally effective. Mr. Frisbee abstained on both votes; Mr. Lewis abstained on the dismissal vote.
title: ZC 3-2-26
